View this page

Connectors¶

Data source connectors for fetching data as Arrow IPC.

DataConnector Protocol¶

from griot_core.connectors import DataConnector

class MyConnector(DataConnector):
    async def fetch_as_arrow(
        self,
        query: str | None = None,
        **kwargs
    ) -> bytes:
        # Return Arrow IPC bytes
        ...

ConnectorRegistry¶

from griot_core.connectors import ConnectorRegistry

registry = ConnectorRegistry()

# Register connector
registry.register("my-source", MyConnector, ...)

# Create connector
connector = registry.create_connector("my-source", config)

# Fetch data
arrow_data = await connector.fetch_as_arrow()

API Reference¶

Griot Core Data Connectors.

This module provides data source connectors that return data in Arrow IPC format for use by executors.

All connectors implement the DataConnector protocol and return Arrow IPC bytes, not DataFrame objects. This keeps griot-core framework-agnostic.

class griot_core.connectors.DataConnector(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Protocol for data source connectors.

All connectors MUST return data in Arrow IPC format. They should NEVER return DataFrames directly.

This keeps griot-core framework-agnostic. The actual DataFrame processing happens inside executors (WASM or Container).

Example implementation:

class SnowflakeConnector:
def __init__(self, config: ConnectorConfig):

self.config = config

async def fetch_as_arrow(

self, schema: Schema, sample_size: Optional[int] = None

) -> bytes:

# Connect to Snowflake async with self._get_connection() as conn:

query = f”SELECT * FROM {schema.physical_name}” if sample_size:

query += f” SAMPLE ({sample_size} ROWS)”

cursor = conn.cursor() cursor.execute(query)

# Snowflake natively supports Arrow arrow_table = cursor.fetch_arrow_all()

# Serialize to IPC format sink = pa.BufferOutputStream() with pa.ipc.new_stream(sink, arrow_table.schema) as writer:

writer.write_table(arrow_table)

return sink.getvalue().to_pybytes()

async def test_connection(self) -> ConnectionTestResult:
try:
async with self._get_connection() as conn:

cursor = conn.cursor() cursor.execute(“SELECT 1”) return ConnectionTestResult(success=True)

except Exception as e:
return ConnectionTestResult(

success=False, errors=[str(e)]

)

abstractmethod async fetch_as_arrow(schema, sample_size=None)[source]¶

Fetch data as Arrow IPC bytes.

This method MUST return Arrow IPC format bytes. It should NOT return a DataFrame.

Parameters:

  • schema (Schema) – The schema defining what to fetch

  • sample_size (int | None) – Optional limit on rows (for sampling)

Returns:

Arrow IPC format bytes

Raises:

  • ConnectionError – If unable to connect to data source

  • QueryError – If the query fails

Return type:

bytes

abstractmethod async fetch_with_query(query, sample_size=None)[source]¶

Fetch data using a custom query.

Parameters:

  • query (str) – SQL query to execute

  • sample_size (int | None) – Optional limit on rows

Returns:

Arrow IPC format bytes

Return type:

bytes

abstractmethod async get_table_schema(physical_name)[source]¶

Get the schema of a table from the data source.

Useful for schema inference and validation.

Parameters:

physical_name (str) – Physical name of the table

Returns:

Dictionary with column names, types, and metadata

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

abstractmethod async test_connection()[source]¶

Test that connection credentials are valid.

Returns:

ConnectionTestResult with success status and any errors

Return type:

ConnectionTestResult

class griot_core.connectors.BaseConnector(config)[source]¶

Base class for connector implementations.

Provides common functionality that all connectors can inherit.

Parameters:

config (ConnectorConfig)

__init__(config)[source]¶

Initialize the connector.

Parameters:

config (ConnectorConfig) – Connector configuration

class griot_core.connectors.ConnectorConfig(connector_type, connection_params=<factory>, timeout_seconds=30, retry_count=3, ssl_enabled=True, extra_options=<factory>)[source]¶

Configuration for a data connector.

This is the base configuration that all connectors share. Specific connectors may have additional fields in their connection parameters.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType)

  • connection_params (Dict[str, Any])

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • retry_count (int)

  • ssl_enabled (bool)

  • extra_options (Dict[str, Any])

connector_type¶

Type of connector (snowflake, bigquery, etc.)

Type:

griot_core.connectors.base.ConnectorType

connection_params¶

Connection-specific parameters

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

timeout_seconds¶

Connection timeout in seconds

Type:

int

retry_count¶

Number of retries for failed connections

Type:

int

ssl_enabled¶

Whether to use SSL

Type:

bool

extra_options¶

Additional connector-specific options

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

retry_count: int = 3¶
ssl_enabled: bool = True¶
timeout_seconds: int = 30¶
connector_type: ConnectorType¶
connection_params: Dict[str, Any]¶
extra_options: Dict[str, Any]¶
class griot_core.connectors.ConnectorType(*values)[source]¶

Types of data source connectors.

SNOWFLAKE = 'snowflake'¶
BIGQUERY = 'bigquery'¶
POSTGRES = 'postgres'¶
MYSQL = 'mysql'¶
REDSHIFT = 'redshift'¶
DATABRICKS = 'databricks'¶
S3_PARQUET = 's3_parquet'¶
GCS_PARQUET = 'gcs_parquet'¶
AZURE_PARQUET = 'azure_parquet'¶
LOCAL_PARQUET = 'local_parquet'¶
LOCAL_CSV = 'local_csv'¶
class griot_core.connectors.ConnectionTestResult(success, message='', latency_ms=None, server_version=None, errors=<factory>)[source]¶

Result of testing a connector’s connection.

Parameters:

  • success (bool)

  • message (str)

  • latency_ms (float | None)

  • server_version (str | None)

  • errors (List[str])

success¶

Whether the connection test succeeded

Type:

bool

message¶

Human-readable message about the result

Type:

str

latency_ms¶

Connection latency in milliseconds

Type:

float | None

server_version¶

Database server version if available

Type:

str | None

errors¶

List of error messages if failed

Type:

List[str]

latency_ms: float | None = None¶
message: str = ''¶
server_version: str | None = None¶
success: bool¶
errors: List[str]¶
class griot_core.connectors.FetchResult(data, row_count, byte_size, fetch_time_ms, truncated=False, warnings=<factory>)[source]¶

Result of fetching data from a connector.

Parameters:

  • data (bytes)

  • row_count (int)

  • byte_size (int)

  • fetch_time_ms (float)

  • truncated (bool)

  • warnings (List[str])

data¶

Arrow IPC format bytes

Type:

bytes

row_count¶

Number of rows fetched

Type:

int

byte_size¶

Size of the data in bytes

Type:

int

fetch_time_ms¶

Time taken to fetch in milliseconds

Type:

float

truncated¶

Whether the data was truncated (sample_size applied)

Type:

bool

warnings¶

Any warnings during fetch

Type:

List[str]

truncated: bool = False¶
data: bytes¶
row_count: int¶
byte_size: int¶
fetch_time_ms: float¶
warnings: List[str]¶
class griot_core.connectors.ConnectorRegistry[source]¶

Registry for data source connectors.

Provides a central registry for registering, discovering, and instantiating data connectors. Each connector type can only be registered once.

Example

>>> registry = ConnectorRegistry()
>>> registry.register(
...     connector_type=ConnectorType.SNOWFLAKE,
...     connector_class=SnowflakeConnector,
...     name="Snowflake",
...     description="Snowflake Data Cloud connector",
...     required_params=["account", "user", "warehouse", "database"],
... )
>>> connector = registry.create_connector(
...     connector_type=ConnectorType.SNOWFLAKE,
...     config=ConnectorConfig(
...         connector_type=ConnectorType.SNOWFLAKE,
...         connection_params={"account": "...", ...}
...     )
... )
__init__()[source]¶

Initialize the connector registry.

Return type:

None

create_connector(connector_type, config)[source]¶

Create a connector instance.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector to create

  • config (ConnectorConfig) – Connector configuration

Returns:

Instantiated connector

Raises:

  • ConnectorNotFoundError – If connector type not registered

  • ValueError – If required parameters are missing

Return type:

DataConnector

create_from_dict(connector_type, connection_params, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a connector from a type string and parameters dict.

Convenience method for creating connectors from configuration files.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (str) – Type string (e.g., “snowflake”, “bigquery”)

  • connection_params (Dict[str, Any]) – Connection parameters

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional ConnectorConfig options

Returns:

Instantiated connector

Raises:

ValueError – If connector type string is invalid

Return type:

DataConnector

get_connector_info(connector_type)[source]¶

Get information about a registered connector.

Parameters:

connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector

Returns:

ConnectorInfo for the connector type

Raises:

ConnectorNotFoundError – If connector type not registered

Return type:

ConnectorInfo

is_registered(connector_type)[source]¶

Check if a connector type is registered.

Parameters:

connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type to check

Returns:

True if registered, False otherwise

Return type:

bool

list_connector_types()[source]¶

List all registered connector types.

Returns:

List of registered ConnectorType values

Return type:

List[ConnectorType]

list_connectors()[source]¶

List all registered connectors.

Returns:

List of ConnectorInfo for all registered connectors

Return type:

List[ConnectorInfo]

register(connector_type, connector_class, name, description='', required_params=None, optional_params=None, supports_sampling=True, supports_arrow_native=False, factory=None)[source]¶

Register a connector type.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector to register

  • connector_class (Type[DataConnector]) – The connector implementation class

  • name (str) – Human-readable name

  • description (str) – Description of the connector

  • required_params (List[str] | None) – Required connection parameters

  • optional_params (Dict[str, Any] | None) – Optional parameters with default values

  • supports_sampling (bool) – Whether the connector supports sampling

  • supports_arrow_native (bool) – Whether the data source has native Arrow support

  • factory (Callable[[ConnectorConfig], DataConnector] | None) – Optional custom factory function for creating instances

Raises:

ConnectorRegistrationError – If connector type already registered

Return type:

None

async test_connector(connector_type, config)[source]¶

Test a connector configuration.

Creates a temporary connector instance and tests the connection.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector

  • config (ConnectorConfig) – Configuration to test

Returns:

ConnectionTestResult with success status

Return type:

ConnectionTestResult

unregister(connector_type)[source]¶

Unregister a connector type.

Parameters:

connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector to unregister

Raises:

ConnectorNotFoundError – If connector type not registered

Return type:

None

validate_config(connector_type, config)[source]¶

Validate a connector configuration.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector

  • config (ConnectorConfig) – Configuration to validate

Returns:

List of validation error messages (empty if valid)

Return type:

List[str]

class griot_core.connectors.ConnectorInfo(connector_type, connector_class, name, description='', required_params=<factory>, optional_params=<factory>, supports_sampling=True, supports_arrow_native=False)[source]¶

Information about a registered connector.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType)

  • connector_class (Type[DataConnector])

  • name (str)

  • description (str)

  • required_params (List[str])

  • optional_params (Dict[str, Any])

  • supports_sampling (bool)

  • supports_arrow_native (bool)

connector_type¶

Type of connector

Type:

griot_core.connectors.base.ConnectorType

connector_class¶

The connector implementation class

Type:

Type[griot_core.connectors.base.DataConnector]

name¶

Human-readable name

Type:

str

description¶

Description of the connector

Type:

str

required_params¶

Required connection parameters

Type:

List[str]

optional_params¶

Optional connection parameters with defaults

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

supports_sampling¶

Whether the connector supports sampling

Type:

bool

supports_arrow_native¶

Whether the data source has native Arrow support

Type:

bool

description: str = ''¶
supports_arrow_native: bool = False¶
supports_sampling: bool = True¶
connector_type: ConnectorType¶
connector_class: Type[DataConnector]¶
name: str¶
required_params: List[str]¶
optional_params: Dict[str, Any]¶
exception griot_core.connectors.ConnectorNotFoundError[source]¶

Raised when a connector type is not registered.

exception griot_core.connectors.ConnectorRegistrationError[source]¶

Raised when connector registration fails.

griot_core.connectors.get_default_registry()[source]¶

Get the default connector registry.

Returns:

The global default ConnectorRegistry instance

Return type:

ConnectorRegistry

griot_core.connectors.register_connector(connector_type, connector_class, name, **kwargs)[source]¶

Register a connector in the default registry.

Convenience function for registering connectors.

Parameters:

  • connector_type (ConnectorType) – Type of connector

  • connector_class (Type[DataConnector]) – Connector implementation class

  • name (str) – Human-readable name

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional registration options

Return type:

None
